summary: On Saturday, Robert Kiyosaki said he bought another full Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) for $67,000 even as he described the cryptocurrency as "crashing," fram

headline: Trump Now Says Global Tariff Will Be 15%, Not 10%
summary: On Saturday, President Donald Trump said in a post on Truth Social that he is lifting a worldwide tariff rate to 15% from 10% and said the change takes effect immediately

headline: Trump Targets Coal Plant Toxin Rules To Boost Power Supply
summary: Trump's EPA plans to ease coal-plant mercury and toxin limits to support power supply, while health advocates warn of higher pollution harms.

headline: PacifiCorp Settles $575 Million Wildfire Claims Amid Financial Pressure
summary: PacifiCorp, which is owned by Berkshire Hathaway, reached a $575 million agreement with the U.S. government on Friday to settle allegations linked to six wildfires in Oregon and California.

headline: Trump Plans March China Visit As Trade And Security Talks Loom
summary: Trump will travel to China from March 31 to April 2 as both countries navigate trade disputes, Taiwan tensions and renewed diplomatic engagement.

headline: Steve Jobs Was Notoriously Demanding But Pixar's Ed Catmull Never Had A Loud Disagreement With The Apple Co-Founder: This Is His Secret
summary: Secret to surviving disagreements with Steve Jobs was waiting them out, not winning arguments in the room. Slow-motion debates shaped better decisions by Pixar and Apple.

headline: 74% Blame Trump's Tariffs For Inflation Surge, Poll Reveals Public Sentiment
summary: According to the YouGov poll a majority of Americans believe that Trump's tariffs have led to increased prices, with 66% indicating that they have experienced a rise in costs for goods.

headline: Trump Orders Major Military Buildup Toward Iran While Advisers Push Economic Agenda
summary: President Donald Trump escalated tensions with Iran by warning of severe consequences if Tehran does not agree to a nuclear deal within 10 to 15 days.

headline: NASA Targets Early March Crewed Moon Flyby Mission
summary: NASA targets an early March Artemis II launch to send astronauts on a historic lunar flyby that will test systems ahead of a future Moon landing.

headline: President Trump, In Truth Social Post, Said "Effective Immediately, Raising The 10% Worldwide Tariff On Countries, Many Of Which Have Been "Ripping" The U.S. Off For Decades, Without Retribution (Until I Came Along!), To The Fully Allowed, And Legally Tested, 15% Level"
Seattle Seahawks For Sale: Will Jeff Bezos, Steve Ballmer Add NFL Team To Investments?
summary: The NFL's Seattle Seahawks are officially up for sale. Likely commanding a record sale price, Jeff Bezos is among the names that could have interest and afford the team.

headline: Weekend Round-Up: Ripple Ceo's Crypto Bill Prediction, Bitcoin's Potential $1 Million Push And More
summary: This week in crypto featured Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse predicting strong odds of a crypto bill's passage, Anthony Pompliano floating Bitcoin's potential $1 million trajectory, the White House taking control of stablecoin legislation talks, Elizabeth Warren clashing with crypto leaders, and Stripe-owned Bridge winning conditional federal trust bank approval.

headline: Elon Musk Is Betting Big On Data Centers In Space, But OpenAI's Sam Altman Just Gave His 'Ridiculous' Idea A Reality Check: 'How Hard It Is To…'
summary: Elon Musk's ambitious plan to deploy massive data centers in orbit is drawing sharp criticism from OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who dismissed the idea as "ridiculous" — at least for now — citing steep costs and logistical nightmares.

headline: Super Micro Computer, Blue Owl, Hims & Hers And More: 5 Stocks Investors Couldn't Stop Buzzing About This Week
summary: Retail investors talked up five hot stocks this week on X and Reddit, driven by hype, earnings, AI, and news flow.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: eBay, Carvana, Devon — And Supreme Court Nixes Trump Tariffs
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors' favorite stocks over the last week — here's a look at some of our top stories.

headline: Trump To Remove Vietnam From Restricted US Tech List, Hanoi Says
summary: President Donald Trump told Vietnam's Communist Party chief To Lam that he will remove the country from a list restricted from accessing advanced U.S. technologies, the Asian nation's government announced on Saturday.

headline: Peter Schiff Says 'No Country Has Been Ripping Off America' As Trump Lashes Out After SCOTUS Tariff Ruling
summary: Peter Schiff criticized U.S. trade policy as President Donald Trump responded to a Supreme Court ruling blocking emergency tariff powers by announcing a new 10% global tariff, even as recent data showed inflation cooled to 2.4% in January.

headline: Gene Munster Predicts Nvidia Growth Will Hit 40% In 2027, Calls Wall Street's 28% Estimate 'Too Conservative' Ahead Of Earnings
summary: Ahead of Nvidia's Feb. 25 earnings report, Deepwater's Gene Munster said Wall Street is underestimating the chipmaker's AI-driven momentum, projecting 40% growth in 2027 versus the Street's 28% estimate as rising AI utility, hyperscaler capex and inference demand support a stronger long-term outlook.

headline: Microsoft Veteran Phil Spencer To Retire As Gaming Unit Chief, Insider Asha Sharma To Take Over
summary: Microsoft Corp. announced that longtime Xbox chief Phil Spencer is retiring after 38 years, with Asha Sharma taking over the gaming division amid declining revenue, post-Activision Blizzard integration and mounting competitive pressure.

headline: Scott Bessent Says 2026 Tariff Revenue 'Virtually Unchanged' After SCOTUS Ruling Sparks Trump's Fury— 'We'll See Whether...'
summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Friday the Trump administration expects U.S. tariff revenue to remain "virtually unchanged" in 2026, even after the Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump's sweeping "reciprocal" import duties.

headline: Mark Zuckerberg's Meta Loses National PTA Partnership As Child Safety Lawsuits And Instagram Addiction Trial Intensify
summary: The National PTA has ended its funding partnership with Meta amid mounting child safety lawsuits and an Instagram addiction trial involving CEO Mark Zuckerberg, even as META stock holds a strong Quality rating but trends negatively across time frames.

headline: Trump To Visit China From March 31–April 2 For High-Stakes Meeting With Xi Jinping As US Supreme Court Overturns Sweeping Tariffs
summary: President Donald Trump will visit Beijing from March 31 to April 2 for high-stakes talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping, just after the Supreme Court of the United States overturned key tariffs central to his trade strategy, as both sides weigh extending a trade truce amid tensions over Taiwan, agriculture and technology restrictions.

headline: Why Anthropic's New AI Tool Claude Code Security Is Rattling Cybersecurity Stocks
summary: On Friday, Anthropic unveiled Claude Code Security, a new AI‑driven tool designed to autonomously hunt down software vulnerabilities and propose fixes.

headline: Elon Musk's Tesla Must Pay $243 Milllion In 2019 Fatal Autopilot Crash As Judge Says Evidence 'More Than Supports' Liability
summary: A Miami federal judge upheld a $243 million verdict against Tesla, Inc. in a 2019 fatal Autopilot crash, rejecting the company's bid for a new trial and dealing another setback as it pushes to expand its robotaxi ambitions.

headline: Luxury EV Maker Lucid Cuts 12% Of Workforce In Profitability Push
summary: Lucid Group is cutting 12% of its global workforce as the luxury EV maker moves to streamline operations and push closer to profitability after a turbulent year.

headline: OpenAI Tempers Compute Spending Target To $600 Billion, Projects $280 Billion Revenue By 2030 As Mega Funding Round Nears: Report
summary: OpenAI now expects to spend $600 billion on computing power through 2030 and clock revenues of $280 billion as a mega-funding round looms.
